The first step is to retrieve the information of the MCMC simulation experiments.
The inputs :

Input files must a name like “xxxxx.1.in”
Output files must a name like “xxxxx.1.out”



Code
path_mod_IMD <- here::here("mod/TK_IMD")
MCMC_out_IMD <- f_MCSim_ind(path_mod_IMD)

NC <- MCMC_out_IMD$NC
Nb_param <- MCMC_out_IMD$Nb_param
Nb_experiement <- MCMC_out_IMD$Nb_experiement
Nb_iter_kept <- MCMC_out_IMD$Nb_iter_kept
N_iter_setpoint <- MCMC_out_IMD$N_iter_setpoint

L_parameters <- unique(MCMC_out_IMD$Priors$Nom)


We can retrieve different information from the model :

The number of chains used : 3
The number of parameters : 6
The number of experiments : 64
The number of last iterations used to describe the posterior distribution : 10^{4}
The number of last iterations used to create the tab_setpoint.out file : 333

Warning : Run the command ./mcsim.m_TK_IMD TK_IMD_Setpoint.in in the terminal while being in the folder mod/TK_IMD to create the Setpoints.out now. Do the same with ./mcsim.m_TK_IMD TK_IMD_Setpoint_ind.in to create the Setpoints_ind.out and with ./mcsim.m_TK_IMD TK_IMD_Setpoint_ind_full.in to create the Setpoints_ind_full.out.

Code
n_samples <- 1e6 # Simulation of the distribution

df_priors_dist <- MCMC_out_IMD$Priors |> 
  dplyr::select(Nom, Distribution, P1, P2, P3, P4) |> 
  rowwise() |> 
  mutate(samples = case_when(
    Distribution == "Uniform" ~ list(runif(n_samples, min = P1, max = P2)),
    Distribution == "Normal" ~ list(rnorm(n_samples, mean = P1, sd = P2)),
    Distribution == "LogUniform" ~ list(exp(runif(n_samples, min = log(P1), max = log(P2)))),
    Distribution == "TruncNormal" ~ list(rtruncnorm(n_samples, a = P3, b = P4, mean = P1, sd = P2))
  )) |> 
  unnest(samples) |> 
  filter(!(Nom %in% c("kuIMD", "keIMD") & samples > 0.05))

col_prior <- Nord_polar[4]
alpha_prior <- 0.5

p_priors <- ggplot() +
  facet_wrap(~Nom, scales = "free")+
  
  stat_halfeye(
    data = df_priors_dist, 
    aes(
       x = samples
       ),
    alpha = alpha_prior,
    fill = col_prior,
    color = col_prior,
    normalize = "xy" 
    ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Priors distributions", 
    x = "Parameter value", 
    y = "Density"
    ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_priors

Code
# Large format per chain
mcmc_list <- lapply(split(MCMC_out_IMD$Chains, MCMC_out_IMD$Chains$Chain), function(d) {
  wide <- d %>%
    dplyr::select(Iteration, Parameter, value) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Parameter, values_from = value) %>%
    arrange(Iteration)
  
  mcmc(as.matrix(wide[,-1]))
})

mcmc_list <- as.mcmc.list(mcmc_list)




Code
  par(mfrow=c(1,3))

  gelman.plot(mcmc_list,
            bin.width = 10,      # Number of observations per segment,
            # first segment --> at least 50 iterations.
            max.bins = 50,       # Maximum number of bins, excluding the last one
            confidence = 0.95,   # Coverage probability of confidence interval.
            transform = FALSE,   # improve the normality of the distribution
            autoburnin=TRUE)     # Remove first half of sequence

  
  gelman <- gelman.diag(mcmc_list, confidence = 0.95, transform=FALSE, autoburnin=TRUE, multivariate=TRUE)
  print(gelman)

Code
MCMC_out_IMD$Chains <- MCMC_out_IMD$Chains |> 
  mutate(Nom = str_remove(Parameter, "\\.\\d+\\.$")) 

f_get_mode <- function(x) {
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

ku_samples <- MCMC_out_IMD$Chains |> filter(Nom == "kuIMD")
ke_samples <- MCMC_out_IMD$Chains |> filter(Nom == "keIMD")

BCF_samples <- ku_samples$value / ke_samples$value

BCF_mode <- f_get_mode(BCF_samples)
BCF_CI <- quantile(BCF_samples, probs = c(0.025, 0.975))
BCF_CI_low <- as.numeric(BCF_CI[1])
BCF_CI_up <- as.numeric(BCF_CI[2])
BCF_min <- min(BCF_samples)
BCF_max <- max(BCF_samples)
BCF_mean <- mean(BCF_samples)
BCF_sd <- sd(BCF_samples)

BCF <- c(
  BCF_mode, 
  BCF_CI_low, BCF_CI_up, 
  BCF_min, BCF_max, 
  BCF_mean, BCF_sd
  )




Code
Summary_res_IMD_tmp <- cbind(as.data.frame(MCMC_out_IMD$Summary_res)[1:length(L_parameters)], BCF)

Summary_res_IMD_f <- Summary_res_IMD_tmp |>

# BIC calculation

LL <- f_get_mode(MCMC_out_IMD$df_LnPost$LnPosterior)

n <- length(na.omit(df_TK_IMD$Weight)) + length(na.omit(df_TK_IMD$CiIMD))
k <- Nb_param -2

BIC <- -2*LL + k*log(n)

cat("n = ", n,"\nk = ", k, "\nLL = ", LL, "\nBIC = ", BIC)

Code
dObs_ind <- df_TK_IMD |> 
  mutate(
    ID = as.numeric(ID),
    Group = case_when(
      ID < 65 ~ "Uptake",
      ID > 64 ~ "Elimination"
    )
    )

colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "Weight"] <- "Weight_obs"
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "CiIMD"] <- "CiIMD_obs"
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "t"] <- "Times"

col_exposure <- c(Nord_aurora[4], Nord_aurora[1], Nord_frost[2])

Sim.Res.ind <- lapply(1:64, function(j) {
  read_tsv(file.path(path_mod_IMD, paste0("Setpoints_ind_", j, ".out"))) %>%
    mutate(sim = j)
}) %>%
  bind_rows()

df_mod_ind <- NULL

for (i in 1:Nb_experiement){
  
  ID_i <- unique(dObs_ind$ID)[i]

  Sim.Res.ind_i  <- subset(Sim.Res.ind, sim == i) |> 
    dplyr::select(where(~ !all(is.na(.x))))
  
  dObs_i <- subset(dObs_ind, ID == ID_i)
  
  Time_pred = dObs_i$Time # !!!
  
  Nom_Endpoints = c("Weight", "CiIMD")
  
  Nsortie = 2
  
  MPV = IC_min = IC_max = Endpoint = index = NULL
  
  for (j in 1:length(Nom_Endpoints)){
    
    index =grep(paste0("^",Nom_Endpoints[j],"_") , colnames(Sim.Res.ind_i), fixed=FALSE)

    
    Data_sim = as.matrix( Sim.Res.ind_i[ , index] )
    
    MPV    = c(  MPV,  as.numeric( Data_sim[nrow(Data_sim), ] ) )
    IC_min = c(IC_min,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.025, na.rm=TRUE) )
    IC_max = c(IC_max,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.975, na.rm=TRUE) ) 
    
    Endpoint = c(Endpoint, rep(Nom_Endpoints[j], ncol(Data_sim)))
  }
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- data.frame(
    predict.endpoint = MPV,
    Time    = rep(Time_pred, Nsortie),
    low     = IC_min,
    up      = IC_max,
    Endpt   = Endpoint
    )
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- df_mod_ind_i %>%
    mutate(ID=ID_i)

  df_mod_ind <- rbind(df_mod_ind, df_mod_ind_i)
}

df_mod_Weight_ind <- subset(df_mod_ind, Endpt == "Weight")
df_mod_CiIMD_ind <- subset(df_mod_ind, Endpt == "CiIMD")

df_predobs_CiIMD_ind <- dObs_ind |> 
  left_join(df_mod_CiIMD_ind, suffix = c("_obs", "_pred")) |> 
  dplyr::select(-c(Weight_obs, CiEPX)) |> 
  mutate(across(everything(), ~replace(.x, .x == -1, NA))) |> 
  drop_na()

df_predobs_Weight_ind <- dObs_ind |> 
  filter(!(expo == 2)) |> 
  left_join(df_mod_Weight_ind, suffix = c("_obs", "_pred"))|>
  dplyr::select(-c(CiIMD_obs, CiEPX)) |> 
  mutate(across(everything(), ~replace(.x, .x == -1, NA))) |> 
  drop_na()

Figure 4.9: Results of the model fit on experimental data. Predicted values are represented with points with their respective credibility interval of 95%. Thin black and grey lines represent the 2-folds and 5-fold changes, respectively. The thicker black line represents the identity line.







Code
df_predobs_Weight_ind <- df_predobs_Weight_ind |>
  mutate(
    Ratio_predobs = predict.endpoint / Weight_obs,
    Residuals = predict.endpoint - Weight_obs
  )

Weight_count_fold2 <- length(subset(df_predobs_Weight_ind, Ratio_predobs >= 0.5 & Ratio_predobs <= 2)$Weight_obs) / length(df_predobs_Weight_ind$Weight_obs) * 100

Weight_count_fold5 <- length(subset(df_predobs_Weight_ind, Ratio_predobs >= 0.2 & Ratio_predobs <= 5)$Weight_obs) / length(df_predobs_Weight_ind$Weight_obs) * 100

df_residuals_CiIMD <- df_predobs_CiIMD_ind |> 
  mutate(
    Residual = predict.endpoint - CiIMD_obs,
    Ratio_Residual = predict.endpoint/CiIMD_obs
    ) |> 
  mutate(
    Time_point = as.numeric(as.character(Time_point_f)),
    Group = factor(Group, c("Uptake", "Elimination")),
    Time_relative = case_when(
      Group == "Uptake" ~ Time_point,
      Group == "Elimination" ~ Time_point - 21

dObs_ind <- df_TK_IMD |> 
  mutate(ID = as.numeric(ID))
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "Weight"] <- "Weight_obs"
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "CiIMD"] <- "CiIMD_obs"
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "t"] <- "Times"

nb_rows_ind <- NC*N_iter_setpoint+1


Sim.Res.ind.full <- lapply(1:64, function(j) {
  read_tsv(file.path(path_mod_IMD, paste0("Setpoints_ind_full_", j, ".out"))) %>%
    mutate(sim = j)
}) %>%
  bind_rows()


df_mod_ind <- NULL

for (i in 1:Nb_experiement){

  
  ID_i <- unique(dObs_ind$ID)[i]

  Sim.Res.ind.full_i  <- subset(Sim.Res.ind.full, sim == i) |> 
    dplyr::select(where(~ !all(is.na(.x))))
  
  dObs_i <- subset(dObs_ind, ID == ID_i)
  
  Nom_Endpoints = c("Weight", "CiIMD", "C_exposure")
  
  Nsortie = length(Nom_Endpoints)
  
  MPV = IC_min = IC_max = Endpoint = index = NULL
  
  for (j in 1:length(Nom_Endpoints)){
    
    index =grep(paste0("^",Nom_Endpoints[j],"_") , colnames(Sim.Res.ind.full_i), fixed=FALSE)

    
    Data_sim = as.matrix( Sim.Res.ind.full_i[ , index] )
    
    MPV    = c(  MPV,  as.numeric( Data_sim[nrow(Data_sim), ] ) )
    IC_min = c(IC_min,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.025, na.rm=TRUE) )
    IC_max = c(IC_max,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.975, na.rm=TRUE) ) 
    
    Endpoint = c(Endpoint, rep(Nom_Endpoints[j], ncol(Data_sim)))
  }
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- data.frame(
    predict.endpoint = MPV,
    Time    = rep(Time_pred, Nsortie),
    low     = IC_min,
    up      = IC_max,
    Endpt   = Endpoint
    )
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- df_mod_ind_i %>%
    mutate(ID=ID_i)

  df_mod_ind <- rbind(df_mod_ind, df_mod_ind_i)
}

y_segment <- 2000

df_phases <- dObs_ind |>
  arrange(ID, Time) |>
  group_by(ID) |>
  mutate(
    row = row_number(),
    t_start = Time,
    t_end = lead(Time),

    y = y_segment

  filter(!is.na(t_end)) |>  
  dplyr::select(ID, segment, t_start, t_end, y) |>
  ungroup() |>

    Phase = case_when(
      (segment == 1 & ID < 65) ~ 1,
      (segment == 2 & ID < 65) ~ 3,
      (segment == 1 & ID > 64) ~ 1,
      (segment == 2 & ID > 64) ~ 2,
      (segment == 3 & ID > 64) ~ 3
    )
  )

title: Toxicokinetics of single substances
text:
Experiment
Research question: What is the kinetic of uptake and depuration of EPX and IMD in earthworms ?

title: Toxicokinetics of single substances
text:
Exposition of earthworms
We aimed to perform toxicokinetic experiements based on OECD TG 317 to be able to estimate the uptake and the depuration rate of each molecule.


Code
t_elim <- 21
t <- seq(0,t_elim*2,0.01)
C0 <- 0
C_soil <- 10
C_soil <- 1000
ks <- 0.1
ke <- 1

fdata <- function(t, state, parameters) {
    with(as.list(c(state, parameters)), {
      if(t<21){dl <- ks*C_soil-ke*state[1]}else{dl <- -ke*state[1]}
      list(c(dl))
    })  }

# Initial condition
init_var <- c(C0)

# Parameters and time
params <- c(
  ks=ks, 
  ke=ke, 
  C_soil=C_soil
  )

# Resolution
sol_fdata <- ode(
  y=init_var, 
  times=t, 
  func=fdata, 
  parms=params
  )
  
sim_data_ex <- data.frame(
  t= sol_fdata[,1], 
  Cworm = sol_fdata[,2]
  )
sim_data_ex_up <- sim_data_ex[sim_data_ex$t<=t_elim,]
sim_data_ex_elim <- sim_data_ex[sim_data_ex$t>=t_elim,]

times_uptake <- c(1, 2, 3, 7, 10, 14, 17, 21)
times_elim <- c(22, 23, 24, 28, 31, 35, 38, 42)
times_points <- c(times_uptake, times_elim)

sim_data_points <- sim_data_ex[sim_data_ex$t %in% times_points,]


p <- ggplot()+
  geom_line(
    aes(
      x=sim_data_ex_up$t, 
      y=sim_data_ex_up$Cworm
      ), 
    color=col_uptake, 
    linewidth=2
    )+
  geom_line(
    aes(
      x=sim_data_ex_elim$t, 
      y=sim_data_ex_elim$Cworm
      ), 
    color=col_elim, 
    linewidth=2
    )+
  geom_point(
    aes(
      x=sim_data_points$t, 
      y=sim_data_points$Cworm
      ), 
    cex=3
    )+
  geom_label_repel(
    aes(x=sim_data_points$t, 
        y=sim_data_points$Cworm, 
        label = sim_data_points$t
        ),
    box.padding   = 0.35, 
    point.padding = 0.5,
    segment.color = Nord_polar[1]
    )+
  annotate(
    "text", 
    x=t_elim/2, 
    y=min(sim_data_ex$Cworm), 
    colour=col_uptake, 
    label="Uptake", 
    fontface="bold"
    )+
  annotate(
    "text", 
    x=t_elim*1.5, 
    y=max(sim_data_ex$Cworm), 
    colour=col_elim, 
    label="Elimination", 
    fontface="bold"
    )+
  
  labs(x="Time (d)", y=expression(paste("Concentration in earthworm")))+
  
  theme_bw()+
  theme(
    legend.position = "right", 
    legend.title=element_text(size=sizetitle-2,face="bold"), 
    title=element_text(size=sizetitle, face="bold"), 
    axis.title.x = element_text(size=sizetitle,face="plain"), 
    axis.title.y = element_text(size=sizetitle,face="plain")
    )

Figure 1: Desing of the toxicokinetic experiements.





The Figure 3.1 gives a general idea of the chosen experimental design with a first phase of uptake when earthworms are exposed to a given dose of a given substance and a second phase of elimination when earthworms are not exposed anymore. Timepoints are defined when the substance concentration in earthworms is measured to be able to describe the uptake and elimination curves (8 timepoints each, in 4 replicates).
One replicate consists of 1 individual exposed to the given concentration during the given time point if the time point is in the uptake phase or during 21 days with an elimination period (non exposed period) until the given time point. Individuals are kept individually in a medium cosm containing 200 g of soil (dry weight) at a WHC of 70% and fed with 3 g/ind/14 d (dry weight) of horse dung (WHC 70%). Cosms are kept in a thermoregulated enclosure at 18°C.
AJOUTER PHOTO COSME AVEC UN VER
The chosen tested concentration is 1 mg/kg for EPX for which no lethal effect is expected.
At the end of their exposure scenario, earthworms are weighted and then put on wet filter paper in Petri dishes for about 24 hours so their can void their gut content. At the end of these 24 hours, they are massaged to removed what is left in the gut and then weighted and frozen at -80°C.


Chemical analysis of soils and earthworms (HPLC-HRMS)
AJOUTER PROTOCOLES

title: Toxicokinetics of single substances
section: Relationship between fresh weight and fresh weight without gut content
text:
Relationship between fresh weight and fresh weight without gut content
Since experimental data include weights with and without gut content, we established a quantitative relationship between them to allow conversion and ensure consistency across datasets.


Code
df_TK_full <- read_excel(here::here("data/Data_TK_long.xlsx")) |> 
  mutate(
    Time_point_f = as.factor(Time_point),
    ID = as.factor(ID),
    w = w/1000, # Conversion mg to g
    Dose = Dose*1000, # mg/kg to ng/g (mg/kg = microg/g = 1000 ng/g),
    CiIMD = C_worm_IMD,
    CiEPX = C_worm_EPX
  ) |> 
  mutate(
    C_soil_IMD = case_when(
      t == 0 & ID_recipient == "1" ~ 0.089, # ng/g
      t == 0 & ID_recipient == "2" ~ 0.072  # ng/g
    ),
    C_soil_EPX = case_when(
      t == 0 & ID_recipient == "1" ~ 0.780, # ng/g
      t == 0 & ID_recipient == "2" ~ 1.070  # ng/g
    )
  ) |> 
  mutate(expo = case_when(
    Phase == "Uptake" ~ 1,
    Phase == "Elimination" ~ 0,
    Phase == "Frozen" ~ 2
    )
  ) |> 
  filter(Keep == "Yes") |> 
  group_by(ID) |> 
  mutate(t = as.numeric(difftime(Date, first(Date), units = "days"))) |> 
  dplyr::ungroup()




Code
# t1 = dernière masse avant expo == 2
t1_df <- df_TK_full %>%
  filter(Phase != "Frozen") %>%
  group_by(ID) %>%
  filter(t == max(t)) %>%
  dplyr::select(ID, w_popo = w, Molecule)

# t2 = première masse pendant expo == 2
t2_df <- df_TK_full %>%
  filter(Phase == "Frozen") %>%
  group_by(ID) %>%
  dplyr::select(ID, w_nopopo = w, Molecule)

# Joindre les deux et calculer le pourcentage de perte
df_TK_fasting <- t1_df %>%
  inner_join(t2_df, by = c("ID", "Molecule")) %>%
  mutate(
    Per_loss = 100 * (w_popo - w_nopopo) / w_popo,
    Loss = (w_popo - w_nopopo)
    )

title: Toxicokinetics of single substances
section: Description of the gut depuration dynamic
text:
Description of the gut depuration dynamic
When on Petri dishes, we need to account for the slow voiding process of the gut content during the 24 hours there are fasting.
A small experiment was done with 10 earthworms individually put in cosm with 200 g of Closeaux soil and 3 g of horse dung. After a week they were placed on filter paper in Petri dishes and weighted regularly. After 32 hours, earthworms were massaged to void the rest of their gut and weighted again.


Code

  mutate(
    ID = as.factor(ID)
  ) |> 
  group_by(ID) |> 
  mutate(
    Per_Weight = 100 * Weight / Weight[t == 0]
    ) |> 
  ungroup()

Figure 2: Evolution of earthworm weight and the corresponding percentage of initial weight throughout time in Petri dishes. Weight after being massaged are described with squared points.





We modele the dynamic with a first order decay :
\[\frac{dP_{Weight}}{dt}=-\frac{\ln 2}{DT_{50,\text{gut}}}\ (P_{Weight}-P^{\infty}_{Weight})\]
\[P_{Weight}=P^{\infty}_{Weight}+(100-P^{\infty}_{Weight})\ \exp\left(- \frac{\ln 2}{DT_{50,\text{gut}}}\times t\right)\]
With :

\(t\) : Time (h)
\(P_{Weight}\) : Percentage of initial weight (%, 0-100)
\(P^{\infty}_{Weight}\) : Percentage of initial weight when gut is fully emptied (%)
\(DT_{50,\text{gut}}\) : Half-life of the gut content (h)

For the model, we consider that weights measured after massage are weights measured after a significant amount of time has passed. Therefore, the corresponding percentages of initial weight inform \(P^{\infty}_{Weight}\).


Code
df_gut_mod <- df_gut |> 
  mutate(
    t = case_when(
      Handling == "None" ~ t,
      Handling == "Massaged" ~ 1000
    )
  )

mod.gut <- nlsLM(Per_Weight ~ Per_Weight_void + (100-Per_Weight_void)*exp(-lambda*t),
               data = df_gut_mod,
               start = list(
                 Per_Weight_void = 15,
                 lambda = 0.01
                 ),
               lower = c(0,0), 
               upper = c(Inf,Inf)
               )

summary(mod.gut)

Figure 3: Modeling of the evolution of earthworm percentage of initial weight throughout time in Petri dishes. The grey line correspond to the prediction of the model.






Toxicokinetics modeling
The dynamic of the internal concentration is modeled with a one compartment model taking dilution with growth into account.
\[
\begin{aligned}
\frac{\mathrm{d}C_i}{\mathrm{d}t}&= \frac1L(k_u\times C_e-k_e\times C_i)-C_i\times \frac1V \frac{\mathrm{d}V}{\mathrm{d}t}\\
&=\frac1L\left(k_u\times C_e-k_e\times C_i-\frac{3a}{L} \ C_i \times \frac{\mathrm{d}L}{\mathrm{d}t} \right)
\end{aligned}
\]

\(C_e\) : Soil concentration (ng/g)
\(C_i\) : Internal concentration in earthworms (ng/g)
\(L\) : Structural volumetric length (cm)
\(V\) : Structural volume (cm\(^3\)) \(= L^3\)
\(k_u\) : Uptake rate (cm.g(soil).day\(^{-1}\).g(worm)\(^{-1}\))
\(k_e\) : Elimination rate (cm.day\(^{-1}\))

Large and weakly informative priors were assigned to the toxicokinetic rate constants in order to cover a wide range of plausible values. Specifically, the uptake (\(k_u\)), elimination (\(k_e\)), and inter-compartmental exchange rates (\(k_{periph}\)) were given log-uniform priors between 10\(^{−5}\) and 10\(^5\), ensuring that the posterior estimates would be primarily informed by the data rather than overly restrictive assumptions. For the growth process, truncated normal priors were specified for both the mean growth rate and its inter-individual variation, reflecting prior biological knowledge of adult earthworm growth while avoiding unrealistic values. Measurement processes were also integrated in the model: observed body weights were assigned truncated normal distributions to ensure positivity and consistency with biological constraints, while internal concentrations were assumed to follow truncated log-normal distributions, which are more appropriate for right-skewed data and guarantee non-negative values.

title: 4  Toxicokinetics of Imidacloprid
text:
4.1.1 Data importation
Notations :

Date_start : Start of the experiement (t0)
Molecule : Substance to which the earthworm is exposed
Dose : Exposure dose (mg/kg)
ID_recipient : Recipient in which the soil was prepared
ID_camp : ID of the earthworm campagne and generation (see XXX for more information)
ID : ID of the earthworm
Nb_rep : Identification number of the replicate
Time_point : Time point to which the earthworm is dedicated
Phase : Uptake or Elimination phase
Time_introduction_D0 : Time of the day when the earthworm was introduced into his contaminated cosm
w0 : Body weight at t0
w_Tp : Body weight at the given time point
w_frozen : Body weight before congelation
C_worm : Measured concentration of the substance in the earthworm
C_soil : Measured concentration of the substance in the soil at the given time point



Code
df_TK_IMD <- f_read_data_TK("IMD") %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ na_if(.x, -1)))


df_TK_IMD <- df_TK_IMD |> 
  mutate(
    Time = t,
    CiEPX = C_worm_EPX,
    CiIMD = C_worm_IMD,
    Weight = Weight_nog,
    Time_point_f = as.factor(Time_point)
  ) |> 
  dplyr::select(Time, CiEPX, CiIMD, Weight, w, Molecule, ID, expo, Time_point_f) |> 
  mutate(
    CiEPX = na_if(CiEPX, -1),
    CiIMD = na_if(CiIMD, -1)
    )

df_TK_IMD_mean <- df_TK_IMD |> 
  dplyr::select(-ID) |> 
  group_by(Time) |> 
  summarise(
    CiEPX = exp(mean(log(CiEPX[CiEPX > 0]), na.rm = TRUE)),
    CiIMD = exp(mean(log(CiIMD[CiIMD > 0]), na.rm = TRUE)),
    Weight = mean(Weight, na.rm = TRUE),
    w = mean(w, na.rm = TRUE),
    expo = mean(expo),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |> 
  mutate(Molecule = "IMD")

Code
n_samples <- 1e6 # Simulation of the distribution

df_priors_dist <- MCMC_out_EPX$Priors |> 
  dplyr::select(Nom, Distribution, P1, P2, P3, P4) |> 
  rowwise() |> 
  mutate(samples = case_when(
    Distribution == "Uniform" ~ list(runif(n_samples, min = P1, max = P2)),
    Distribution == "Normal" ~ list(rnorm(n_samples, mean = P1, sd = P2)),
    Distribution == "LogUniform" ~ list(exp(runif(n_samples, min = log(P1), max = log(P2)))),
    Distribution == "TruncNormal" ~ list(rtruncnorm(n_samples, a = P3, b = P4, mean = P1, sd = P2))
  )) |> 
  unnest(samples) |> 
  filter(!(Nom %in% c("kuEPX", "keEPX","kperiph") & samples > 0.01))

col_prior <- Nord_polar[4]
alpha_prior <- 0.5

p_priors <- ggplot() +
  facet_wrap(~Nom, scales = "free")+
  
  stat_halfeye(
    data = df_priors_dist, 
    aes(
       x = samples
       ),
    alpha = alpha_prior,
    fill = col_prior,
    color = col_prior,
    normalize = "xy" 
    ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Priors distributions", 
    x = "Parameter value", 
    y = "Density"
    ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_priors 








Figure 5.1: Distributions of the priors.

Code
# Large format per chain
mcmc_list <- lapply(split(MCMC_out_EPX$Chains, MCMC_out_EPX$Chains$Chain), function(d) {
  wide <- d %>%
    dplyr::select(Iteration, Parameter, value) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Parameter, values_from = value) %>%
    arrange(Iteration)
  
  mcmc(as.matrix(wide[,-1]))
})

mcmc_list <- as.mcmc.list(mcmc_list)




Code
  par(mfrow=c(1,3))

  gelman.plot(mcmc_list,
            bin.width = 10,      # Number of observations per segment,
            # first segment --> at least 50 iterations.
            max.bins = 50,       # Maximum number of bins, excluding the last one
            confidence = 0.95,   # Coverage probability of confidence interval.
            transform = FALSE,   # improve the normality of the distribution
            autoburnin=TRUE)     # Remove first half of sequence

  
  gelman <- gelman.diag(mcmc_list, confidence = 0.95, transform=FALSE, autoburnin=TRUE, multivariate=TRUE)
  print(gelman)

title: 5  Toxicokinetics of Epoxiconazole - 2 compartment & No exposure in Petri dishes
text:
5.3 Parameter estimation results


Code
alpha_prior <- 0.5
alpha_post <- 0.5

Code
f_get_mode <- function(x) {
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

MCMC_out_EPX$Chains <- MCMC_out_EPX$Chains |> 
  mutate(Nom = str_remove(Parameter, "\\.\\d+\\.$"))

ku_samples <- MCMC_out_EPX$Chains |> filter(Nom == "kuEPX")
ke_samples <- MCMC_out_EPX$Chains |> filter(Nom == "keEPX")

BCF_samples <- ku_samples$value / ke_samples$value

BCF_mode <- f_get_mode(BCF_samples)
BCF_CI <- quantile(BCF_samples, probs = c(0.025, 0.975))
BCF_CI_low <- as.numeric(BCF_CI[1])
BCF_CI_up <- as.numeric(BCF_CI[2])
BCF_min <- min(BCF_samples)
BCF_max <- max(BCF_samples)
BCF_mean <- mean(BCF_samples)
BCF_sd <- sd(BCF_samples)

BCF <- c(
  BCF_mode, 
  BCF_CI_low, BCF_CI_up, 
  BCF_min, BCF_max, 
  BCF_mean, BCF_sd
  )

dObs_ind <- df_TK_EPX |> 
  mutate(
    ID = as.numeric(ID),
    Group = case_when(
      ID < 65 ~ "Uptake",
      ID > 64 ~ "Elimination"
    )
    )

colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "Weight"] <- "Weight_obs"
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "CiEPX"] <- "CiEPX_obs"
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "t"] <- "Times"

col_exposure <- c(Nord_aurora[4], Nord_aurora[1], Nord_frost[2])

Sim.Res.ind <- lapply(1:62, function(j) {
  read_tsv(file.path(path_mod_EPX, paste0("Setpoints_ind_", j, ".out"))) %>%
    mutate(sim = j)
}) %>%
  bind_rows()

df_mod_ind <- NULL

for (i in 1:Nb_experiement){
  
  ID_i <- unique(dObs_ind$ID)[i]

  Sim.Res.ind_i  <- subset(Sim.Res.ind, sim == i) |> 
    dplyr::select(where(~ !all(is.na(.x))))
  
  dObs_i <- subset(dObs_ind, ID == ID_i)
  
  Time_pred = dObs_i$Time # !!!
  
  Nom_Endpoints = c("Weight", "CiEPX")
  
  Nsortie = 2
  
  MPV = IC_min = IC_max = Endpoint = index = NULL
  
  for (j in 1:length(Nom_Endpoints)){
    
    index =grep(paste0("^",Nom_Endpoints[j],"_") , colnames(Sim.Res.ind_i), fixed=FALSE)

    
    Data_sim = as.matrix( Sim.Res.ind_i[ , index] )
    
    MPV    = c(  MPV,  as.numeric( Data_sim[nrow(Data_sim), ] ) )
    IC_min = c(IC_min,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.025, na.rm=TRUE) )
    IC_max = c(IC_max,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.975, na.rm=TRUE) ) 
    
    Endpoint = c(Endpoint, rep(Nom_Endpoints[j], ncol(Data_sim)))
  }
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- data.frame(
    predict.endpoint = MPV,
    Time    = rep(Time_pred, Nsortie),
    low     = IC_min,
    up      = IC_max,
    Endpt   = Endpoint
    )
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- df_mod_ind_i %>%
    mutate(ID=ID_i)

  df_mod_ind <- rbind(df_mod_ind, df_mod_ind_i)
}

df_mod_Weight_ind <- subset(df_mod_ind, Endpt == "Weight")
df_mod_CiEPX_ind <- subset(df_mod_ind, Endpt == "CiEPX")

df_predobs_CiEPX_ind <- dObs_ind |> 
  left_join(df_mod_CiEPX_ind, suffix = c("_obs", "_pred")) |> 
  dplyr::select(-c(Weight_obs, CiIMD)) |> 
  mutate(across(everything(), ~replace(.x, .x == -1, NA))) |> 
  drop_na()

  dplyr::select(-c(CiEPX_obs, CiIMD)) |> 
  mutate(across(everything(), ~replace(.x, .x == -1, NA))) |>

y_segment <- 3600

df_phases <- dObs_ind |>
  arrange(ID, Time) |>
  group_by(ID) |>
  mutate(
    row = row_number(),
    t_start = Time,
    t_end = lead(Time),  
    segment = row,
    y = y_segment
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(t_end)) |>  
  dplyr::select(ID, segment, t_start, t_end, y) |>
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    Phase = case_when(
      (segment == 1 & ID < 65) ~ 1,
      (segment == 2 & ID < 65) ~ 3,
      (segment == 1 & ID > 64) ~ 1,
      (segment == 2 & ID > 64) ~ 2,
      (segment == 3 & ID > 64) ~ 3
    )
  )

text:
5.6 Simulation of the toxicodynamic in earthworms throughout the exposition
The goal here is to recreate the concentration dynamic in the average earthworm during our exposition scheme but without the fasting phase that was required for chemical analysis and during which elimination was happening.

Sim.Res <- as.data.frame(fread(here::here(path_mod_EPX, "Setpoints.out"),sep="\t"))

  
  Nom_Endpoints =c("Weight_1", "CiEPX_1", "C_exposure_1")
  Nsortie = 3
  MPV = IC_min = IC_max = Endpoint = index = NULL

  for (i in 1:length(Nom_Endpoints)){
    
    index =grep(paste("^",Nom_Endpoints[i],"\\.", sep="") , colnames(Sim.Res), fixed=FALSE )
    
    Data_sim = as.matrix( Sim.Res[ , index] )
    
    MPV    = c(  MPV,  as.numeric( Data_sim[nrow(Data_sim), ] ) )
    IC_min = c(IC_min,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.025) )
    IC_max = c(IC_max,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.975) ) 
    
    Endpoint = c(Endpoint, rep(Nom_Endpoints[i], ncol(Data_sim)))

    }

  df_mod <- data.frame(
    predict.endpoint = MPV,
    Time    = c( rep(Time_pred, Nsortie)),
    low     = IC_min,
    up      = IC_max,
    Endpt   = Endpoint,
    Experiment = c(rep(rep("Experience 1", length(Time_pred)), Nsortie))
    )
  
df_mod_Weight <- subset(df_mod, Endpt == "Weight_1")
df_mod_CiEPX <- subset(df_mod, Endpt == "CiEPX_1")

Code
n_samples <- 1e6 # Simulation of the distribution

df_priors_dist <- MCMC_out_EPX$Priors |> 
  dplyr::select(Nom, Distribution, P1, P2, P3, P4) |> 
  rowwise() |> 
  mutate(samples = case_when(
    Distribution == "Uniform" ~ list(runif(n_samples, min = P1, max = P2)),
    Distribution == "Normal" ~ list(rnorm(n_samples, mean = P1, sd = P2)),
    Distribution == "LogUniform" ~ list(exp(runif(n_samples, min = log(P1), max = log(P2)))),
    Distribution == "TruncNormal" ~ list(rtruncnorm(n_samples, a = P3, b = P4, mean = P1, sd = P2))
  )) |> 
  unnest(samples) |> 
  filter(!(Nom %in% c("kuEPX", "keEPX","kperiph") & samples > 0.01))

col_prior <- Nord_polar[4]
alpha_prior <- 0.5

p_priors <- ggplot() +
  facet_wrap(~Nom, scales = "free")+
  
  stat_halfeye(
    data = df_priors_dist, 
    aes(
       x = samples
       ),
    alpha = alpha_prior,
    fill = col_prior,
    color = col_prior,
    normalize = "xy" 
    ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Priors distributions", 
    x = "Parameter value", 
    y = "Density"
    ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_priors

mcmc_list <- lapply(split(MCMC_out_EPX$Chains, MCMC_out_EPX$Chains$Chain), function(d) {
  wide <- d %>%
    dplyr::select(Iteration, Parameter, value) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Parameter, values_from = value) %>%
    arrange(Iteration)
  
  mcmc(as.matrix(wide[,-1]))
})

mcmc_list <- as.mcmc.list(mcmc_list)

  par(mfrow=c(1,3))

f_get_mode <- function(x) {
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

MCMC_out_EPX$Chains <- MCMC_out_EPX$Chains |> 
  mutate(Nom = str_remove(Parameter, "\\.\\d+\\.$"))

ku_samples <- MCMC_out_EPX$Chains |> filter(Nom == "kuEPX")
ke_samples <- MCMC_out_EPX$Chains |> filter(Nom == "keEPX")

BCF_samples <- ku_samples$value / ke_samples$value

BCF_mode <- f_get_mode(BCF_samples)
BCF_CI <- quantile(BCF_samples, probs = c(0.025, 0.975))
BCF_CI_low <- as.numeric(BCF_CI[1])
BCF_CI_up <- as.numeric(BCF_CI[2])
BCF_min <- min(BCF_samples)
BCF_max <- max(BCF_samples)
BCF_mean <- mean(BCF_samples)
BCF_sd <- sd(BCF_samples)

BCF <- c(
  BCF_mode, 
  BCF_CI_low, BCF_CI_up, 
  BCF_min, BCF_max, 
  BCF_mean, BCF_sd
  )

dObs_ind <- df_TK_EPX |> 
  mutate(
    ID = as.numeric(ID),
    Group = case_when(
      ID < 65 ~ "Uptake",
      ID > 64 ~ "Elimination"
    )
    )

colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "Weight"] <- "Weight_obs"
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "CiEPX"] <- "CiEPX_obs"
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "t"] <- "Times"

col_exposure <- c(Nord_aurora[4], Nord_aurora[1], Nord_frost[2])

Sim.Res.ind <- lapply(1:62, function(j) {
  read_tsv(file.path(path_mod_EPX, paste0("Setpoints_ind_", j, ".out"))) %>%
    mutate(sim = j)
}) %>%
  bind_rows()

df_mod_ind <- NULL

for (i in 1:Nb_experiement){
  
  ID_i <- unique(dObs_ind$ID)[i]

  Sim.Res.ind_i  <- subset(Sim.Res.ind, sim == i) |> 
    dplyr::select(where(~ !all(is.na(.x))))
  
  dObs_i <- subset(dObs_ind, ID == ID_i)
  
  Time_pred = dObs_i$Time # !!!
  
  Nom_Endpoints = c("Weight", "CiEPX")
  
  Nsortie = 2
  
  MPV = IC_min = IC_max = Endpoint = index = NULL
  
  for (j in 1:length(Nom_Endpoints)){
    
    index =grep(paste0("^",Nom_Endpoints[j],"_") , colnames(Sim.Res.ind_i), fixed=FALSE)

    
    Data_sim = as.matrix( Sim.Res.ind_i[ , index] )
    
    MPV    = c(  MPV,  as.numeric( Data_sim[nrow(Data_sim), ] ) )
    IC_min = c(IC_min,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.025, na.rm=TRUE) )
    IC_max = c(IC_max,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.975, na.rm=TRUE) ) 
    
    Endpoint = c(Endpoint, rep(Nom_Endpoints[j], ncol(Data_sim)))
  }
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- data.frame(
    predict.endpoint = MPV,
    Time    = rep(Time_pred, Nsortie),
    low     = IC_min,
    up      = IC_max,
    Endpt   = Endpoint
    )
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- df_mod_ind_i %>%
    mutate(ID=ID_i)

  df_mod_ind <- rbind(df_mod_ind, df_mod_ind_i)
}

df_mod_Weight_ind <- subset(df_mod_ind, Endpt == "Weight")
df_mod_CiEPX_ind <- subset(df_mod_ind, Endpt == "CiEPX")

Code
dObs_ind <- df_TK_EPX |> 
  mutate(ID = as.numeric(ID))
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "Weight"] <- "Weight_obs"
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "CiEPX"] <- "CiEPX_obs"
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "t"] <- "Times"

nb_rows_ind <- NC*N_iter_setpoint+1


Sim.Res.ind.full <- lapply(1:62, function(j) {
  read_tsv(file.path(path_mod_EPX, paste0("Setpoints_ind_full_", j, ".out"))) %>%
    mutate(sim = j)
}) %>%
  bind_rows()


df_mod_ind <- NULL

for (i in 1:Nb_experiement){
  
  ID_i <- unique(dObs_ind$ID)[i]

  Sim.Res.ind.full_i  <- subset(Sim.Res.ind.full, sim == i) |> 
    dplyr::select(where(~ !all(is.na(.x))))
  
  dObs_i <- subset(dObs_ind, ID == ID_i)
  
  Nom_Endpoints = c("Weight", "CiEPX", "C_exposure")
  
  Nsortie = length(Nom_Endpoints)
  
  MPV = IC_min = IC_max = Endpoint = index = NULL
  
  for (j in 1:length(Nom_Endpoints)){
    
    index =grep(paste0("^",Nom_Endpoints[j],"_") , colnames(Sim.Res.ind.full_i), fixed=FALSE)

    
    Data_sim = as.matrix( Sim.Res.ind.full_i[ , index] )
    
    MPV    = c(  MPV,  as.numeric( Data_sim[nrow(Data_sim), ] ) )
    IC_min = c(IC_min,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.025, na.rm=TRUE) )
    IC_max = c(IC_max,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.975, na.rm=TRUE) ) 
    
    Endpoint = c(Endpoint, rep(Nom_Endpoints[j], ncol(Data_sim)))
  }
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- data.frame(
    predict.endpoint = MPV,
    Time    = rep(Time_pred, Nsortie),
    low     = IC_min,
    up      = IC_max,
    Endpt   = Endpoint
    )
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- df_mod_ind_i %>%
    mutate(ID=ID_i)

  df_mod_ind <- rbind(df_mod_ind, df_mod_ind_i)
}

df_mod_Weight_ind <- subset(df_mod_ind, Endpt == "Weight")
df_mod_CiEPX_ind <- subset(df_mod_ind, Endpt == "CiEPX")
df_mod_CeEPX_ind <- subset(df_mod_ind, Endpt == "C_exposure")

time_limits <- dObs_ind %>%
  group_by(ID) %>%
  summarise(time_cutoff = max(Time, na.rm = TRUE) + 5)

df_mod_CiEPX_ind_t <- df_mod_CiEPX_ind %>%
  left_join(time_limits, by = "ID") %>%
  filter(Time <= time_cutoff) %>%
  dplyr::select(-time_cutoff) 

df_mod_Weight_ind_t <- df_mod_Weight_ind %>%
  left_join(time_limits, by = "ID") %>%
  filter(Time <= time_cutoff) %>%
  dplyr::select(-time_cutoff) 

df_mod_CeEPX_ind_t <- df_mod_CeEPX_ind %>%
  left_join(time_limits, by = "ID") %>%
  filter(Time <= time_cutoff) %>%
  dplyr::select(-time_cutoff) 




Code
y_segment <- 3600

df_phases <- dObs_ind |>
  arrange(ID, Time) |>
  group_by(ID) |>
  mutate(
    row = row_number(),
    t_start = Time,
    t_end = lead(Time),  
    segment = row,
    y = y_segment
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(t_end)) |>  
  dplyr::select(ID, segment, t_start, t_end, y) |>
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    Phase = case_when(
      (segment == 1 & ID < 65) ~ 1,
      (segment == 2 & ID < 65) ~ 3,
      (segment == 1 & ID > 64) ~ 1,
      (segment == 2 & ID > 64) ~ 2,
      (segment == 3 & ID > 64) ~ 3
    )
  )

text:
6.6 Simulation of the toxicodynamic in earthworms throughout the exposition
The goal here is to recreate the concentration dynamic in the average earthworm during our exposition scheme but without the fasting phase that was required for chemical analysis and during which elimination was happening.

Sim.Res <- as.data.frame(fread(here::here(path_mod_EPX, "Setpoints.out"),sep="\t"))

    low     = IC_min,

Code
n_samples <- 1e6 # Simulation of the distribution

df_priors_dist <- MCMC_out_EPX$Priors |> 
  dplyr::select(Nom, Distribution, P1, P2, P3, P4) |> 
  rowwise() |> 
  mutate(samples = case_when(
    Distribution == "Uniform" ~ list(runif(n_samples, min = P1, max = P2)),
    Distribution == "Normal" ~ list(rnorm(n_samples, mean = P1, sd = P2)),
    Distribution == "LogUniform" ~ list(exp(runif(n_samples, min = log(P1), max = log(P2)))),
    Distribution == "TruncNormal" ~ list(rtruncnorm(n_samples, a = P3, b = P4, mean = P1, sd = P2))
  )) |> 
  unnest(samples) |> 
  filter(!(Nom %in% c("kuEPX", "keEPX","kperiph") & samples > 0.01))

col_prior <- Nord_polar[4]
alpha_prior <- 0.5

p_priors <- ggplot() +
  facet_wrap(~Nom, scales = "free")+
  
  stat_halfeye(
    data = df_priors_dist, 
    aes(
       x = samples
       ),
    alpha = alpha_prior,
    fill = col_prior,
    color = col_prior,
    normalize = "xy" 
    ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Priors distributions", 
    x = "Parameter value", 
    y = "Density"
    ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_priors

f_get_mode <- function(x) {
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

MCMC_out_EPX$Chains <- MCMC_out_EPX$Chains |> 
  mutate(Nom = str_remove(Parameter, "\\.\\d+\\.$"))

ku_samples <- MCMC_out_EPX$Chains |> filter(Nom == "kuEPX")
ke_samples <- MCMC_out_EPX$Chains |> filter(Nom == "keEPX")

BCF_samples <- ku_samples$value / ke_samples$value

BCF_mode <- f_get_mode(BCF_samples)
BCF_CI <- quantile(BCF_samples, probs = c(0.025, 0.975))
BCF_CI_low <- as.numeric(BCF_CI[1])
BCF_CI_up <- as.numeric(BCF_CI[2])
BCF_min <- min(BCF_samples)
BCF_max <- max(BCF_samples)
BCF_mean <- mean(BCF_samples)
BCF_sd <- sd(BCF_samples)

BCF <- c(
  BCF_mode, 
  BCF_CI_low, BCF_CI_up, 
  BCF_min, BCF_max, 
  BCF_mean, BCF_sd
  )

dObs_ind <- df_TK_EPX |> 
  mutate(
    ID = as.numeric(ID),
    Group = case_when(
      ID < 65 ~ "Uptake",
      ID > 64 ~ "Elimination"
    )
    )

colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "Weight"] <- "Weight_obs"
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "CiEPX"] <- "CiEPX_obs"
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "t"] <- "Times"

col_exposure <- c(Nord_aurora[4], Nord_aurora[1], Nord_frost[2])

Sim.Res.ind <- lapply(1:62, function(j) {
  read_tsv(file.path(path_mod_EPX, paste0("Setpoints_ind_", j, ".out"))) %>%
    mutate(sim = j)
}) %>%
  bind_rows()

df_mod_ind <- NULL

for (i in 1:Nb_experiement){
  
  ID_i <- unique(dObs_ind$ID)[i]

  Sim.Res.ind_i  <- subset(Sim.Res.ind, sim == i) |> 
    dplyr::select(where(~ !all(is.na(.x))))
  
  dObs_i <- subset(dObs_ind, ID == ID_i)
  
  Time_pred = dObs_i$Time # !!!
  
  Nom_Endpoints = c("Weight", "CiEPX")
  
  Nsortie = 2
  
  MPV = IC_min = IC_max = Endpoint = index = NULL
  
  for (j in 1:length(Nom_Endpoints)){
    
    index =grep(paste0("^",Nom_Endpoints[j],"_") , colnames(Sim.Res.ind_i), fixed=FALSE)

    
    Data_sim = as.matrix( Sim.Res.ind_i[ , index] )
    
    MPV    = c(  MPV,  as.numeric( Data_sim[nrow(Data_sim), ] ) )
    IC_min = c(IC_min,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.025, na.rm=TRUE) )
    IC_max = c(IC_max,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.975, na.rm=TRUE) ) 
    
    Endpoint = c(Endpoint, rep(Nom_Endpoints[j], ncol(Data_sim)))
  }
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- data.frame(
    predict.endpoint = MPV,
    Time    = rep(Time_pred, Nsortie),
    low     = IC_min,
    up      = IC_max,
    Endpt   = Endpoint
    )
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- df_mod_ind_i %>%
    mutate(ID=ID_i)

  df_mod_ind <- rbind(df_mod_ind, df_mod_ind_i)
}

df_mod_Weight_ind <- subset(df_mod_ind, Endpt == "Weight")
df_mod_CiEPX_ind <- subset(df_mod_ind, Endpt == "CiEPX")

df_predobs_CiEPX_ind <- dObs_ind |> 
  left_join(df_mod_CiEPX_ind, suffix = c("_obs", "_pred")) |> 
  dplyr::select(-c(Weight_obs, CiIMD)) |> 
  mutate(across(everything(), ~replace(.x, .x == -1, NA))) |> 
  drop_na()

df_predobs_Weight_ind <- dObs_ind |> 
  filter(!(expo == 2)) |>

  dplyr::select(-c(CiEPX_obs, CiIMD)) |> 
  mutate(across(everything(), ~replace(.x, .x == -1, NA))) |> 
  drop_na()

y_segment <- 3600

df_phases <- dObs_ind |>
  arrange(ID, Time) |>
  group_by(ID) |>
  mutate(
    row = row_number(),
    t_start = Time,
    t_end = lead(Time),  
    segment = row,
    y = y_segment
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(t_end)) |>  
  dplyr::select(ID, segment, t_start, t_end, y) |>
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    Phase = case_when(
      (segment == 1 & ID < 65) ~ 1,
      (segment == 2 & ID < 65) ~ 3,
      (segment == 1 & ID > 64) ~ 1,
      (segment == 2 & ID > 64) ~ 2,
      (segment == 3 & ID > 64) ~ 3
    )
  )

section: 7.6 Simulation of the toxicodynamic in earthworms throughout the exposition
text:
7.6 Simulation of the toxicodynamic in earthworms throughout the exposition
The goal here is to recreate the concentration dynamic in the average earthworm during our exposition scheme but without the fasting phase that was required for chemical analysis and during which elimination was happening.


Code
Time_min <- 0
Time_max <- 42
Time_step <- 0.25
Time_pred <- seq(Time_min, Time_max, Time_step)
print_times <- paste(Time_min, Time_max, Time_step, sep=", ")

l_param_name_tot <- "kuEPX, keEPX, kperiph, a_growth, Vr_a_growth, Sigma_W, Sigma_CiEPX"

f_Setpoint(path_mod_EPX, Molecule, print_times, l_param_name_tot)

Code
Sim.Res <- as.data.frame(fread(here::here(path_mod_EPX, "Setpoints.out"),sep="\t"))

  
  Nom_Endpoints =c("Weight_1", "CiEPX_1", "C_exposure_1")
  Nsortie = 3
  MPV = IC_min = IC_max = Endpoint = index = NULL

  for (i in 1:length(Nom_Endpoints)){
    
    index =grep(paste("^",Nom_Endpoints[i],"\\.", sep="") , colnames(Sim.Res), fixed=FALSE )
    
    Data_sim = as.matrix( Sim.Res[ , index] )
    
    MPV    = c(  MPV,  as.numeric( Data_sim[nrow(Data_sim), ] ) )
    IC_min = c(IC_min,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.025) )
    IC_max = c(IC_max,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.975) ) 
    
    Endpoint = c(Endpoint, rep(Nom_Endpoints[i], ncol(Data_sim)))

    }

  df_mod <- data.frame(
    predict.endpoint = MPV,
    Time    = c( rep(Time_pred, Nsortie)),
    low     = IC_min,
    up      = IC_max,
    Endpt   = Endpoint,
    Experiment = c(rep(rep("Experience 1", length(Time_pred)), Nsortie))
    )
  
df_mod_Weight <- subset(df_mod, Endpt == "Weight_1")
df_mod_CiEPX <- subset(df_mod, Endpt == "CiEPX_1") 
  
  # length(MPV)
  # length(df_Cin$Time)
  # length(Endpoint)
  # length(df_Cin$Experiment)

title: 4  Toxicokinetics of Epoxiconazole - One compartment & No exposure in Petri dishes
text:
4.1.1 Data importation
Notations :

Date_start : Start of the experiement (t0)
Molecule : Substance to which the earthworm is exposed
Dose : Exposure dose (mg/kg)
ID_recipient : Recipient in which the soil was prepared
ID_camp : ID of the earthworm campagne and generation (see XXX for more information)
ID : ID of the earthworm
Nb_rep : Identification number of the replicate
Time_point : Time point to which the earthworm is dedicated
Phase : Uptake or Elimination phase
Time_introduction_D0 : Time of the day when the earthworm was introduced into his contaminated cosm
w0 : Body weight at t0
w_Tp : Body weight at the given time point
w_frozen : Body weight before congelation
C_worm : Measured concentration of the substance in the earthworm
C_soil : Measured concentration of the substance in the soil at the given time point



Code
df_TK_EPX <- f_read_data_TK("EPX") %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ na_if(.x, -1)))


df_TK_EPX <- df_TK_EPX |> 
  mutate(
    Time = t,
    CiIMD = C_worm_IMD,
    CiEPX = C_worm_EPX,
    Weight = Weight_nog,
    Time_point_f = as.factor(Time_point)
  ) |> 
  dplyr::select(Time, CiEPX, CiIMD, Weight, w, Molecule, ID, expo, Time_point_f) |> 
  mutate(
    CiEPX = na_if(CiEPX, -1),
    CiIMD = na_if(CiIMD, -1)
    )

df_TK_EPX_mean <- df_TK_EPX |> 
  dplyr::select(-ID) |> 
  group_by(Time) |> 
  summarise(
    CiEPX = exp(mean(log(CiEPX[CiEPX > 0]), na.rm = TRUE)),
    CiIMD = exp(mean(log(CiIMD[CiIMD > 0]), na.rm = TRUE)),
    Weight = mean(Weight, na.rm = TRUE),
    w = mean(w, na.rm = TRUE),
    expo = mean(expo),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |> 
  mutate(Molecule = "EPX")

title: 4  Toxicokinetics of Epoxiconazole - One compartment & No exposure in Petri dishes
text:
4.2 Modeling of the toxicokinetics


Code
Molecule <- "EPX"
path_mod_EPX <- here::here("mod/TK_EPX")
path_fig <- here::here("fig/TK_single/EPX_1Comp/")

Nb_Iter <- 100000
seeds <- c(C1 = 3333, C2 = 6656, C3 = 1242)

text_priors <- "Distrib(kuEPX,     LogUniform,   0.00001, 10000); # min, max 
Distrib(keEPX,    LogUniform,   0.00001, 10000); # min, max

Distrib(a_growth,  TruncNormal,   0.005,   0.005,    -0.5,    1); # mean, sd, min, max
Distrib (Vr_a_growth,  TruncNormal, 0.0015, 0.0015, 0, 1);

Distrib(Sigma_W,     TruncNormal,  0.03, 0.01, 0.0001, 1);
Distrib(Sigma_CiEPX, TruncNormal,  3.0, 3.0,    1, 1000);"

text_likelihood <- "Likelihood(CiEPX,  LogNormal, Prediction(CiEPX),  Sigma_CiEPX);  # Calcul des vraisemblances (geometric mean, geometric standard deviation)
Likelihood(Weight, Normal,    Prediction(Weight), Sigma_W);      # Calcul des vraisemblances"

text_param_ind <- "Distrib(a_growth,  TruncNormal, a_growth,  Vr_a_growth, -1.0, 1);"

f_In_tot(path_mod_EPX, Molecule, text_priors, text_likelihood, text_param_ind, Nb_Iter, seeds)




Code
pwd
cd ..
cd ..
cd mod/TK_EPX
pwd

makemcsim m_TK_EPX.model &
wait
./mcsim.m_TK_EPX TK_EPX_C1.in &
./mcsim.m_TK_EPX TK_EPX_C2.in &
./mcsim.m_TK_EPX TK_EPX_C3.in &
wait

<!-- or -->

<!-- makemcsim m_TK_EPX.model & -->
<!-- for j in $(seq 1 62); do -->
<!-- ./mcsim.m_TK_EPX TK_EPX_C${j}.in -->
<!-- done -->


The first step is to retrieve the information of the MCMC simulation experiments.
The inputs :

Input files must a name like “xxxxx.1.in”
Output files must a name like “xxxxx.1.out”



Code
MCMC_out_EPX <- f_MCSim_ind(path_mod_EPX)

NC <- MCMC_out_EPX$NC
Nb_param <- MCMC_out_EPX$Nb_param
Nb_experiement <- MCMC_out_EPX$Nb_experiement
Nb_iter_kept <- MCMC_out_EPX$Nb_iter_kept
N_iter_setpoint <- MCMC_out_EPX$N_iter_setpoint

L_parameters <- unique(MCMC_out_EPX$Priors$Nom)


We can retrieve different information from the model :

The number of chains used : 3
The number of parameters : 6
The number of experiments : 62
The number of last iterations used to describe the posterior distribution : 10^{4}
The number of last iterations used to create the tab_setpoint.out file : 333

Warning : Run the command ./mcsim.m_TK_EPX TK_EPX_Setpoint.in in the terminal while being in the folder mod/TK_EPX to create the Setpoints.out now. Do the same with ./mcsim.m_TK_EPX TK_EPX_Setpoint_ind.in to create the Setpoints_ind.out and with ./mcsim.m_TK_EPX TK_EPX_Setpoint_ind_full.in to create the Setpoints_ind_full.out.

4.2.1 Priors used for the model

MCMC_out_EPX$Priors


          Nom Distribution      P1      P2     P3   P4
1       kuEPX   LogUniform 0.00001 1.0e+04     NA   NA
2       keEPX   LogUniform 0.00001 1.0e+04     NA   NA
3    a_growth  TruncNormal 0.00500 5.0e-03 -5e-01    1
4 Vr_a_growth  TruncNormal 0.00150 1.5e-03  0e+00    1
5     Sigma_W  TruncNormal 0.03000 1.0e-02  1e-04    1
6 Sigma_CiEPX  TruncNormal 3.00000 3.0e+00  1e+00 1000




Code
n_samples <- 1e6 # Simulation of the distribution

df_priors_dist <- MCMC_out_EPX$Priors |> 
  dplyr::select(Nom, Distribution, P1, P2, P3, P4) |> 
  rowwise() |> 
  mutate(samples = case_when(
    Distribution == "Uniform" ~ list(runif(n_samples, min = P1, max = P2)),
    Distribution == "Normal" ~ list(rnorm(n_samples, mean = P1, sd = P2)),
    Distribution == "LogUniform" ~ list(exp(runif(n_samples, min = log(P1), max = log(P2)))),
    Distribution == "TruncNormal" ~ list(rtruncnorm(n_samples, a = P3, b = P4, mean = P1, sd = P2))
  )) |> 
  unnest(samples) |> 
  filter(!(Nom %in% c("kuEPX", "keEPX","kcomp_in", "kcomp_out") & samples > 0.01))

col_prior <- Nord_polar[4]
alpha_prior <- 0.5

p_priors <- ggplot() +
  facet_wrap(~Nom, scales = "free")+
  
  stat_halfeye(
    data = df_priors_dist, 
    aes(
       x = samples
       ),
    alpha = alpha_prior,
    fill = col_prior,
    color = col_prior,
    normalize = "xy" 
    ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Priors distributions", 
    x = "Parameter value", 
    y = "Density"
    ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_priors 








Figure 4.4: Distributions of the priors.

f_get_mode <- function(x) {
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

MCMC_out_EPX$Chains <- MCMC_out_EPX$Chains |> 
  mutate(Nom = str_remove(Parameter, "\\.\\d+\\.$"))

ku_samples <- MCMC_out_EPX$Chains |> filter(Nom == "kuEPX")
ke_samples <- MCMC_out_EPX$Chains |> filter(Nom == "keEPX")

BCF_samples <- ku_samples$value / ke_samples$value

BCF_mode <- f_get_mode(BCF_samples)
BCF_CI <- quantile(BCF_samples, probs = c(0.025, 0.975))
BCF_CI_low <- as.numeric(BCF_CI[1])
BCF_CI_up <- as.numeric(BCF_CI[2])
BCF_min <- min(BCF_samples)
BCF_max <- max(BCF_samples)
BCF_mean <- mean(BCF_samples)
BCF_sd <- sd(BCF_samples)

BCF <- c(
  BCF_mode, 
  BCF_CI_low, BCF_CI_up, 
  BCF_min, BCF_max, 
  BCF_mean, BCF_sd
  )

dObs_ind <- df_TK_EPX |> 
  mutate(

      ID > 64 ~ "Elimination"
    )
    )

colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "CiEPX"] <- "CiEPX_obs"

Sim.Res.ind <- lapply(1:62, function(j) {
  read_tsv(file.path(path_mod_EPX, paste0("Setpoints_ind_", j, ".out"))) %>%
    mutate(sim = j)
}) %>%
  bind_rows()

df_mod_ind <- NULL

for (i in 1:Nb_experiement){
  
  ID_i <- unique(dObs_ind$ID)[i]

  Sim.Res.ind_i  <- subset(Sim.Res.ind, sim == i) |> 
    dplyr::select(where(~ !all(is.na(.x))))
  
  dObs_i <- subset(dObs_ind, ID == ID_i)
  
  Time_pred = dObs_i$Time # !!!
  
  Nom_Endpoints = c("Weight", "CiEPX")
  
  Nsortie = 2
  
  MPV = IC_min = IC_max = Endpoint = index = NULL
  
  for (j in 1:length(Nom_Endpoints)){
    
    index =grep(paste0("^",Nom_Endpoints[j],"_") , colnames(Sim.Res.ind_i), fixed=FALSE)

    
    Data_sim = as.matrix( Sim.Res.ind_i[ , index] )
    
    MPV    = c(  MPV,  as.numeric( Data_sim[nrow(Data_sim), ] ) )
    IC_min = c(IC_min,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.025, na.rm=TRUE) )
    IC_max = c(IC_max,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.975, na.rm=TRUE) ) 
    
    Endpoint = c(Endpoint, rep(Nom_Endpoints[j], ncol(Data_sim)))
  }
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- data.frame(
    predict.endpoint = MPV,
    Time    = rep(Time_pred, Nsortie),
    low     = IC_min,
    up      = IC_max,
    Endpt   = Endpoint
    )
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- df_mod_ind_i %>%
    mutate(ID=ID_i)

  df_mod_ind <- rbind(df_mod_ind, df_mod_ind_i)
}

y_segment <- 1000

df_phases <- dObs_ind |>
  arrange(ID, Time) |>
  group_by(ID) |>
  mutate(
    row = row_number(),
    t_start = Time,
    t_end = lead(Time),  
    segment = row,
    y = y_segment
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(t_end)) |>  
  dplyr::select(ID, segment, t_start, t_end, y) |>
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    Phase = case_when(
      (segment == 1 & ID < 65) ~ 1,
      (segment == 2 & ID < 65) ~ 3,
      (segment == 1 & ID > 64) ~ 1,
      (segment == 2 & ID > 64) ~ 2,
      (segment == 3 & ID > 64) ~ 3
    )
  )

4.2.6 Simulation of the toxicodynamic in earthworms throughout the exposition
The goal here is to recreate the concentration dynamic in the average earthworm during our exposition scheme but without the fasting phase that was required for chemical analysis and during which elimination was happening.


Code
Time_min <- 0
Time_max <- 42
Time_step <- 0.25
Time_pred <- seq(Time_min, Time_max, Time_step)
print_times <- paste(Time_min, Time_max, Time_step, sep=", ")

l_param_name_tot <- "kuEPX, keEPX, a_growth, Vr_a_growth, Sigma_W, Sigma_CiEPX"

f_Setpoint(path_mod_EPX, Molecule, print_times, l_param_name_tot)

Code
Sim.Res <- as.data.frame(fread(here::here(path_mod_EPX, "Setpoints.out"),sep="\t"))

  
  Nom_Endpoints =c("Weight_1", "CiEPX_1", "C_exposure_1")
  Nsortie = 3
  MPV = IC_min = IC_max = Endpoint = index = NULL

  for (i in 1:length(Nom_Endpoints)){
    
    index =grep(paste("^",Nom_Endpoints[i],"\\.", sep="") , colnames(Sim.Res), fixed=FALSE )
    
    Data_sim = as.matrix( Sim.Res[ , index] )
    
    MPV    = c(  MPV,  as.numeric( Data_sim[nrow(Data_sim), ] ) )
    IC_min = c(IC_min,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.025) )
    IC_max = c(IC_max,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.975) ) 
    
    Endpoint = c(Endpoint, rep(Nom_Endpoints[i], ncol(Data_sim)))

    }

  df_mod <- data.frame(
    predict.endpoint = MPV,
    Time    = c( rep(Time_pred, Nsortie)),
    low     = IC_min,
    up      = IC_max,
    Endpt   = Endpoint,
    Experiment = c(rep(rep("Experience 1", length(Time_pred)), Nsortie))
    )
  
df_mod_Weight <- subset(df_mod, Endpt == "Weight_1")
df_mod_CiEPX <- subset(df_mod, Endpt == "CiEPX_1") 
  
  # length(MPV)
  # length(df_Cin$Time)
  # length(Endpoint)
  # length(df_Cin$Experiment)

title: 11  Is there an interaction between IMD & EPX at the TK level ?
text:
11.1 Data importation
We want to answer the following question : Can we predict the concentrations in EPX and IMD in earthworms exposed to different ratios of the mixture ?
Code
df_TK_mix <- f_read_data_TK_mix() |> 
  mutate(
    Ratio_plot = case_when(
      Ratio == "E" ~ "1:0 (EPX only)",
      Ratio == "F" ~ "3:1",
      Ratio == "G" ~ "1:1",
      Ratio == "H" ~ "1:3",
      Ratio == "I" ~ "0:1 (IMD only)",
    )
  )

Ci0IMD <- mean(subset(df_TK_mix, Ratio == "E")$CiIMD, na.rm=T)
Ci0EPX <- mean(subset(df_TK_mix, Ratio == "I")$CiEPX, na.rm=T)

df_TK_mix_mean <- subset(df_TK_mix, t>0) |> 
  group_by(Ratio) |> 
  mutate(
    Dose_IMD = mean(Dose_IMD, na.rm=T),
    Dose_EPX = mean(Dose_EPX, na.rm=T),
    BAFIMD_obs = mean(CiIMD/Dose_IMD, na.rm=T),
    BAFEPX_obs = mean(CiEPX/Dose_EPX, na.rm=T)
  ) |> 
  ungroup(Ratio)
Code
pIMD <- ggplot(
  data = subset(df_TK_mix, t>0),
  aes(
    x = Ratio,
    y = CiIMD
  )
)+
  geom_dotplot(
    aes(
    color=Ratio, 
    fill=Ratio
    ),
    binaxis='y', 
    stackdir='center', 
    dotsize=1, 
    alpha=0.7
    )+
  scale_color_manual(values = Nord_aurora)+
  scale_fill_manual(values = Nord_aurora)+
  theme_minimal()

pEPX <- ggplot(
  data = subset(df_TK_mix, t>0),
  aes(
    x = Ratio,
    y = CiEPX
  )
)+
  geom_dotplot(
    aes(
    color=Ratio, 
    fill=Ratio
    ),
    binaxis='y', 
    stackdir='center', 
    dotsize=1, 
    alpha=0.7
    )+
  scale_color_manual(values = Nord_aurora)+
  scale_fill_manual(values = Nord_aurora)+
  theme_minimal()

pIMDe <- ggplot(
  data = subset(df_TK_mix_mean, (No_vdt==1 & Nb_rep == 1  & t==28)),
  aes(
    x = Ratio,
    y = Dose_IMD
  )
)+
  geom_col(
    aes(
    color=Ratio, 
    fill=Ratio
    ),
    alpha=0.7
    )+
  geom_text(
    aes(
      label = signif(BAFIMD_obs, 3),
      color = Ratio,
      y = max(Dose_IMD) * 1.02
      ),
    vjust = 0,         
    size = 3            
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = Nord_aurora)+
  scale_fill_manual(values = Nord_aurora)+
  theme_minimal()

pEPXe <- ggplot(
  data = subset(df_TK_mix_mean,  (No_vdt==1 & Nb_rep == 1 & t==28)),
  aes(
    x = Ratio,
    y = Dose_EPX
  )
)+
  geom_col(
    aes(
    color=Ratio, 
    fill=Ratio
    ),
    alpha=0.7
    )+
  geom_text(
    aes(
      label = signif(BAFEPX_obs, 3),
      color = Ratio,
      y = max(Dose_EPX) * 1.02
      ),
    vjust = 0,         
    size = 3            
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = Nord_aurora)+
  scale_fill_manual(values = Nord_aurora)+
  theme_minimal()

p <- (pIMD + pEPX)/(pIMDe + pEPXe)
p

crumbs: 6  Toxicokinetics of Epoxiconazole - One compartment & Exposure in Petri dishes (Gut route = 15%)

title: 6  Toxicokinetics of Epoxiconazole - One compartment & Exposure in Petri dishes (Gut route = 15%)
text:
6.1.1 Data importation
Notations :

Date_start : Start of the experiement (t0)
Molecule : Substance to which the earthworm is exposed
Dose : Exposure dose (mg/kg)
ID_recipient : Recipient in which the soil was prepared
ID_camp : ID of the earthworm campagne and generation (see XXX for more information)
ID : ID of the earthworm
Nb_rep : Identification number of the replicate
Time_point : Time point to which the earthworm is dedicated
Phase : Uptake or Elimination phase
Time_introduction_D0 : Time of the day when the earthworm was introduced into his contaminated cosm
w0 : Body weight at t0
w_Tp : Body weight at the given time point
w_frozen : Body weight before congelation
C_worm : Measured concentration of the substance in the earthworm
C_soil : Measured concentration of the substance in the soil at the given time point



Code
df_TK_EPX <- f_read_data_TK("EPX") %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ na_if(.x, -1)))


df_TK_EPX <- df_TK_EPX |> 
  mutate(
    Time = t,
    CiIMD = C_worm_IMD,
    CiEPX = C_worm_EPX,
    Weight = Weight_nog,
    Time_point_f = as.factor(Time_point)
  ) |> 
  dplyr::select(Time, CiEPX, CiIMD, Weight, w, Molecule, ID, expo, Time_point_f) |> 
  mutate(
    CiEPX = na_if(CiEPX, -1),
    CiIMD = na_if(CiIMD, -1)
    )

df_TK_EPX_mean <- df_TK_EPX |> 
  dplyr::select(-ID) |> 
  group_by(Time) |> 
  summarise(
    CiEPX = exp(mean(log(CiEPX[CiEPX > 0]), na.rm = TRUE)),
    CiIMD = exp(mean(log(CiIMD[CiIMD > 0]), na.rm = TRUE)),
    Weight = mean(Weight, na.rm = TRUE),
    w = mean(w, na.rm = TRUE),
    expo = mean(expo),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |> 
  mutate(Molecule = "EPX")

title: 6  Toxicokinetics of Epoxiconazole - One compartment & Exposure in Petri dishes (Gut route = 15%)
text:
6.2 Modeling of the toxicokinetics


Code
Molecule <- "EPX"
path_mod_EPX <- here::here("mod/TK_EPX_15")
path_fig <- here::here("fig/TK_single/EPX_1comp15/")

Nb_Iter <- 100000
seeds <- c(C1 = 3333, C2 = 6654, C3 = 1210)

text_priors <- "Distrib(kuEPX,     LogUniform,   0.00001, 10000); # min, max 
Distrib(keEPX,    LogUniform,   0.00001, 10000); # min, max

Distrib(a_growth,  TruncNormal,   0.005,   0.005,    -0.5,    1); # mean, sd, min, max
Distrib (Vr_a_growth,  TruncNormal, 0.0015, 0.0015, 0, 1);

Distrib(Sigma_W,     TruncNormal,  0.03, 0.01, 0.0001, 1);
Distrib(Sigma_CiEPX, TruncNormal,  3.0, 3.0,    1, 1000);"

text_likelihood <- "Likelihood(CiEPX,  LogNormal, Prediction(CiEPX),  Sigma_CiEPX);  # Calcul des vraisemblances (geometric mean, geometric standard deviation)
Likelihood(Weight, Normal,    Prediction(Weight), Sigma_W);      # Calcul des vraisemblances"

text_param_ind <- "Distrib(a_growth,  TruncNormal, a_growth,  Vr_a_growth, -1.0, 1);"

f_In_tot(path_mod_EPX, Molecule, text_priors, text_likelihood, text_param_ind, Nb_Iter, seeds)

The first step is to retrieve the information of the MCMC simulation experiments.
The inputs :

Input files must a name like “xxxxx.1.in”
Output files must a name like “xxxxx.1.out”



Code

NC <- MCMC_out_EPX$NC

N_iter_setpoint <- MCMC_out_EPX$N_iter_setpoint

L_parameters <- unique(MCMC_out_EPX$Priors$Nom)


We can retrieve different information from the model :

The number of chains used : 3
The number of parameters : 6
The number of experiments : 62
The number of last iterations used to describe the posterior distribution : 10^{4}
The number of last iterations used to create the tab_setpoint.out file : 333

Warning : Run the command ./mcsim.m_TK_EPX TK_EPX_Setpoint.in in the terminal while being in the folder mod/TK_EPX to create the Setpoints.out now. Do the same with ./mcsim.m_TK_EPX TK_EPX_Setpoint_ind.in to create the Setpoints_ind.out and with ./mcsim.m_TK_EPX TK_EPX_Setpoint_ind_full.in to create the Setpoints_ind_full.out.

Code
n_samples <- 1e6 # Simulation of the distribution

df_priors_dist <- MCMC_out_EPX$Priors |> 
  dplyr::select(Nom, Distribution, P1, P2, P3, P4) |> 
  rowwise() |> 
  mutate(samples = case_when(
    Distribution == "Uniform" ~ list(runif(n_samples, min = P1, max = P2)),
    Distribution == "Normal" ~ list(rnorm(n_samples, mean = P1, sd = P2)),
    Distribution == "LogUniform" ~ list(exp(runif(n_samples, min = log(P1), max = log(P2)))),
    Distribution == "TruncNormal" ~ list(rtruncnorm(n_samples, a = P3, b = P4, mean = P1, sd = P2))
  )) |> 
  unnest(samples) |> 
  filter(!(Nom %in% c("kuEPX", "keEPX","kcomp_in", "kcomp_out") & samples > 0.01))

col_prior <- Nord_polar[4]
alpha_prior <- 0.5

p_priors <- ggplot() +
  facet_wrap(~Nom, scales = "free")+
  
  stat_halfeye(
    data = df_priors_dist, 
    aes(
       x = samples
       ),
    alpha = alpha_prior,
    fill = col_prior,
    color = col_prior,
    normalize = "xy" 
    ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Priors distributions", 
    x = "Parameter value", 
    y = "Density"
    ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_priors

mcmc_list <- lapply(split(MCMC_out_EPX$Chains, MCMC_out_EPX$Chains$Chain), function(d) {

Code
f_get_mode <- function(x) {
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

MCMC_out_EPX$Chains <- MCMC_out_EPX$Chains |> 
  mutate(Nom = str_remove(Parameter, "\\.\\d+\\.$"))

ku_samples <- MCMC_out_EPX$Chains |> filter(Nom == "kuEPX")
ke_samples <- MCMC_out_EPX$Chains |> filter(Nom == "keEPX")

BCF_samples <- ku_samples$value / ke_samples$value

BCF_mode <- f_get_mode(BCF_samples)
BCF_CI <- quantile(BCF_samples, probs = c(0.025, 0.975))
BCF_CI_low <- as.numeric(BCF_CI[1])
BCF_CI_up <- as.numeric(BCF_CI[2])
BCF_min <- min(BCF_samples)
BCF_max <- max(BCF_samples)
BCF_mean <- mean(BCF_samples)
BCF_sd <- sd(BCF_samples)

BCF <- c(
  BCF_mode, 
  BCF_CI_low, BCF_CI_up, 
  BCF_min, BCF_max, 
  BCF_mean, BCF_sd
  )




Code
Summary_res_EPX_tmp <- cbind(as.data.frame(MCMC_out_EPX$Summary_res)[1:length(L_parameters)], BCF)

dObs_ind <- df_TK_EPX |> 
  mutate(
    ID = as.numeric(ID),
    Group = case_when(
      ID < 65 ~ "Uptake",
      ID > 64 ~ "Elimination"
    )
    )

colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "Weight"] <- "Weight_obs"
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "CiEPX"] <- "CiEPX_obs"
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "t"] <- "Times"

col_exposure <- c(Nord_aurora[4], Nord_aurora[1], Nord_frost[2])

Sim.Res.ind <- lapply(1:62, function(j) {
  read_tsv(file.path(path_mod_EPX, paste0("Setpoints_ind_", j, ".out"))) %>%
    mutate(sim = j)
}) %>%
  bind_rows()

df_mod_ind <- NULL

for (i in 1:Nb_experiement){
  
  ID_i <- unique(dObs_ind$ID)[i]

  Sim.Res.ind_i  <- subset(Sim.Res.ind, sim == i) |> 
    dplyr::select(where(~ !all(is.na(.x))))
  
  dObs_i <- subset(dObs_ind, ID == ID_i)
  
  Time_pred = dObs_i$Time # !!!
  
  Nom_Endpoints = c("Weight", "CiEPX")
  
  Nsortie = 2
  
  MPV = IC_min = IC_max = Endpoint = index = NULL
  
  for (j in 1:length(Nom_Endpoints)){
    
    index =grep(paste0("^",Nom_Endpoints[j],"_") , colnames(Sim.Res.ind_i), fixed=FALSE)

    
    Data_sim = as.matrix( Sim.Res.ind_i[ , index] )
    
    MPV    = c(  MPV,  as.numeric( Data_sim[nrow(Data_sim), ] ) )
    IC_min = c(IC_min,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.025, na.rm=TRUE) )
    IC_max = c(IC_max,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.975, na.rm=TRUE) ) 
    
    Endpoint = c(Endpoint, rep(Nom_Endpoints[j], ncol(Data_sim)))
  }
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- data.frame(
    predict.endpoint = MPV,
    Time    = rep(Time_pred, Nsortie),
    low     = IC_min,
    up      = IC_max,
    Endpt   = Endpoint
    )
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- df_mod_ind_i %>%
    mutate(ID=ID_i)

  df_mod_ind <- rbind(df_mod_ind, df_mod_ind_i)
}

df_mod_Weight_ind <- subset(df_mod_ind, Endpt == "Weight")

y_segment <- 500

df_phases <- dObs_ind |>
  arrange(ID, Time) |>
  group_by(ID) |>
  mutate(
    row = row_number(),
    t_start = Time,
    t_end = lead(Time),  
    segment = row,
    y = y_segment
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(t_end)) |>  
  dplyr::select(ID, segment, t_start, t_end, y) |>
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    Phase = case_when(
      (segment == 1 & ID < 65) ~ 1,
      (segment == 2 & ID < 65) ~ 3,
      (segment == 1 & ID > 64) ~ 1,
      (segment == 2 & ID > 64) ~ 2,
      (segment == 3 & ID > 64) ~ 3
    )
  )

crumbs: 6  Toxicokinetics of Epoxiconazole - One compartment & Exposure in Petri dishes (Gut route = 15%)

title: 3  Materiel & Methods
text:
3.1 Laboratory experiment
Research question: What is the kinetic of uptake and elimination of EPX and IMD in earthworms ?
Hypothese: Differences in physicochemical properties—epoxiconazole being more lipophilic and imidacloprid more hydrophilic—are expected to produce distinct toxicokinetic profiles and to result in greater bioaccumulation of epoxiconazole.
Prediction: Under co-exposure, the presence of epoxiconazole is expected to reduce the elimination rate of imidacloprid, resulting in higher tissue concentrations of this compound.

title: 3  Materiel & Methods
text:
3.1.1 Exposition of earthworms
Toxicokinetic experiments followed an adapted OECD Test Guideline 317 protocol to determine uptake and elimination rates of each pesticide in earthworms. The experimental design consisted of two sequential phases: a 21-day uptake phase where earthworms were exposed to pesticide-spiked soil, followed by a 21-day elimination phase where individuals were transferred to control soil for depuration.
Sampling was conducted at eight timepoints during each phase: days 1, 2, 3, 7, 10, 14, 17, and 21 for uptake, and days 22, 23, 24, 28, 31, 35, 38, and 42 for elimination (Figure 3.1).


Code
t_elim <- 21
t <- seq(0,t_elim*2,0.01)
C0 <- 0
C_soil <- 10
C_soil <- 1000
ks <- 0.1
ke <- 1

fdata <- function(t, state, parameters) {
    with(as.list(c(state, parameters)), {
      if(t<21){dl <- ks*C_soil-ke*state[1]}else{dl <- -ke*state[1]}
      list(c(dl))
    })  }

# Initial condition
init_var <- c(C0)

# Parameters and time
params <- c(
  ks=ks, 
  ke=ke, 
  C_soil=C_soil
  )

# Resolution
sol_fdata <- ode(
  y=init_var, 
  times=t, 
  func=fdata, 
  parms=params
  )
  
sim_data_ex <- data.frame(
  t= sol_fdata[,1], 
  Cworm = sol_fdata[,2]
  )
sim_data_ex_up <- sim_data_ex[sim_data_ex$t<=t_elim,]
sim_data_ex_elim <- sim_data_ex[sim_data_ex$t>=t_elim,]

times_uptake <- c(1, 2, 3, 7, 10, 14, 17, 21)
times_elim <- c(22, 23, 24, 28, 31, 35, 38, 42)
times_points <- c(times_uptake, times_elim)

sim_data_points <- sim_data_ex[sim_data_ex$t %in% times_points,]


p <- ggplot()+
  geom_line(
    aes(
      x=sim_data_ex_up$t, 
      y=sim_data_ex_up$Cworm
      ), 
    color=col_uptake, 
    linewidth=2
    )+
  geom_line(
    aes(
      x=sim_data_ex_elim$t, 
      y=sim_data_ex_elim$Cworm
      ), 
    color=col_elim, 
    linewidth=2
    )+
  geom_point(
    aes(
      x=sim_data_points$t, 
      y=sim_data_points$Cworm
      ), 
    cex=3
    )+
  geom_label_repel(
    aes(x=sim_data_points$t, 
        y=sim_data_points$Cworm, 
        label = sim_data_points$t
        ),
    box.padding   = 0.35, 
    point.padding = 0.5,
    segment.color = Nord_polar[1]
    )+
  annotate(
    "text", 
    x=t_elim/2, 
    y=min(sim_data_ex$Cworm), 
    colour=col_uptake, 
    label="Uptake", 
    fontface="bold"
    )+
  annotate(
    "text", 
    x=t_elim*1.5, 
    y=max(sim_data_ex$Cworm), 
    colour=col_elim, 
    label="Elimination", 
    fontface="bold"
    )+
  
  labs(x="Time (d)", y=expression(paste("Concentration in earthworm")))+
  
  theme_bw()+
  theme(
    legend.position = "right", 
    legend.title=element_text(size=sizetitle-2,face="bold"), 
    title=element_text(size=sizetitle, face="bold"), 
    axis.title.x = element_text(size=sizetitle,face="plain"), 
    axis.title.y = element_text(size=sizetitle,face="plain")
    )

p








Figure 3.1: Desing of the toxicokinetic experiements.





Each replicate consisted of a single earthworm maintained in an individual plastic vessel (11 cm × 7.7 cm × 4.5 cm) containing 200 g dry weight equivalent of soil adjusted to 70% water holding capacity. This design enabled individual-level measurements of both growth and internal concentrations throughout the experiment. Organisms were fed 3 g dry weight of horse dung per individual every 14 days and maintained at 18°C in a temperature-controlled environment. Individual body weights were recorded at exposure initiation and at respective sampling timepoints. At each timepoint, four individual earthworms were starved for approximately 24 hours on wet filter paper in Petri dishes to clear gut contents, then weighted and sacrificed to be stored at -80°C for subsequent chemical analysis. When the 24 hours were not enough to clear gut content, individuals were massaged to clear the remaining of the gut content.
Test concentrations were set at 100 ng/g for imidacloprid and 1000 ng/g for epoxiconazole, selected to avoid acute toxicity while ensuring analytical quantifiability. These concentrations represent environmentally relevant exposure levels, approximating maximum concentrations reported in French agricultural soils (Pelosi et al., 2021), providing an optimal balance between analytical detectability and ecological relevance.


3.1.2 Chemical analysis of soils and earthworms (HPLC-HRMS)
AJOUTER PROTOCOLES

title: 3  Materiel & Methods
section: 3.2 Relationship between fresh weight and fresh weight without gut content
text:
3.2 Relationship between fresh weight and fresh weight without gut content
Since experimental data include weights with and without gut content, we established a quantitative relationship between them to allow conversion and ensure consistency across datasets.


Code
df_TK_full <- read_excel(here::here("data/Data_TK_long.xlsx")) |> 
  mutate(
    Time_point_f = as.factor(Time_point),
    ID = as.factor(ID),
    w = w/1000, # Conversion mg to g
    Dose = Dose*1000, # mg/kg to ng/g (mg/kg = microg/g = 1000 ng/g),
    CiIMD = C_worm_IMD,
    CiEPX = C_worm_EPX
  ) |> 
  mutate(
    C_soil_IMD = case_when(
      t == 0 & ID_recipient == "1" ~ 0.089, # ng/g
      t == 0 & ID_recipient == "2" ~ 0.072  # ng/g
    ),
    C_soil_EPX = case_when(
      t == 0 & ID_recipient == "1" ~ 0.780, # ng/g
      t == 0 & ID_recipient == "2" ~ 1.070  # ng/g
    )
  ) |> 
  mutate(expo = case_when(
    Phase == "Uptake" ~ 1,
    Phase == "Elimination" ~ 0,
    Phase == "Frozen" ~ 2
    )
  ) |> 
  filter(Keep == "Yes") |> 
  group_by(ID) |> 
  mutate(t = as.numeric(difftime(Date, first(Date), units = "days"))) |> 
  dplyr::ungroup()

  filter(Phase != "Frozen") %>%
  group_by(ID) %>%
  filter(t == max(t)) %>%
  dplyr::select(ID, w_popo = w, Molecule)

# t2 = première masse pendant expo == 2
t2_df <- df_TK_full %>%
  filter(Phase == "Frozen") %>%
  group_by(ID) %>%
  dplyr::select(ID, w_nopopo = w, Molecule)

# Joindre les deux et calculer le pourcentage de perte
df_TK_fasting <- t1_df %>%
  inner_join(t2_df, by = c("ID", "Molecule")) %>%
  mutate(
    Per_loss = 100 * (w_popo - w_nopopo) / w_popo,
    Loss = (w_popo - w_nopopo)
    )

3.2.1 Toxicokinetics modeling

title: 3  Materiel & Methods
3  Materiel & Methods
text:
3.4 Two-compartment model
Because epoxiconazole is a lipophilic substance, a two-compartment model, built upon the one-compartment model, was also tested. In this extended representation, the earthworm body is considered as consisting of a central compartment (e.g., blood or well-perfused tissues) and a peripheral compartment (e.g., storage or poorly perfused tissues), with bidirectional exchanges between them. Uptake from soil occurs only into the central compartment, from which the substance can either be eliminated to the external medium or distributed to the peripheral compartment. The peripheral compartment acts as a storage site that can delay elimination and buffer fluctuations in exposure, which is particularly relevant for hydrophobic compounds expected to partition into lipid-rich tissues. Such a structure allows us to account for a biphasic accumulation and elimination pattern, typically characterized by a fast initial phase driven by the central compartment and a slower phase reflecting the exchange with the peripheral compartment. The internal concentration is now described by Equation 3.4, where the dynamics of the central and peripheral compartments are jointly expressed, enabling a more mechanistic representation of toxicokinetics for lipophilic pesticides in earthworms.
\[
\begin{aligned}
& C_{\text{int},\mu} = C_{\text{int,central},\mu} + C_{\text{int,periph},\mu}\\ \\
&\frac{dC_{\text{int,central},\mu}}{dt} = \frac{1}{L_{i,\mu}} (k_u\ C_{\text{ext}}-k_e\ C_{\text{int,central},\mu}+k_{periph}\ C_{\text{int,periph},\mu}-k_{periph}\ C_{\text{int,central},\mu})-\frac{3a_i}{L_{i,\mu}}C_{\text{int,central},\mu}, \\
&\text{ with}\ \  C_{\text{int,central},\mu}(t=0) = \frac 1 2 C_{\text{int},\mu}^0\\ \\
&\frac{dC_{\text{int,periph},\mu}}{dt} = \frac{1}{L_{i,\mu}} (k_{periph}\ C_{\text{int,central},\mu}-k_{periph}\ C_{\text{int,periph},\mu})-\frac{3a_i}{L_{i,\mu}}C_{\text{int,periph},\mu}, \\
&\text{ with}\ \  C_{\text{int,periph},\mu}(t=0) = \frac 1 2 C_{\text{int},\mu}^0
\end{aligned}
\tag{3.4}\]

title: 3  Materiel & Methods
section: 3.5 Exposure through gut content while in Petri dishes ?
text:
3.5 Exposure through gut content while in Petri dishes ?
Before freezing, earthworms were transferred to Petri dishes lined with moist filter paper for about a day to allow gut clearance and to avoid measuring soil residues in the digestive tract. During this depuration period, worms were no longer in direct contact with contaminated soil; however, residual dietary exposure may still occur due to the ingestion of contaminated soil particles retained in the gut. This exposure pathway is considered negligible for hydrophilic compounds (i.e. imidacloprid) but can represent a significant fraction of the total uptake for hydrophobic substances (log Kow > 4.5), with reported contributions ranging from 15 to 40% (belfroid_1995?; jager_2003a?). Epoxiconazole is moderately hydrophobic (log Kow = 3.3), for which little information is available.
To evaluate the potential influence of this pathway, we tested alternative model scenarios assuming a digestive contribution of 0%, 15%, and 30% of the total uptake. Depuration of the earthworm gut content was modeled as a first-order decay (Equation 3.5), informed by a small supporting experiment: ten earthworms were kept individually in 200 g of Closeaux soil with 3 g of horse dung for one week, then transferred to Petri dishes lined with moist filter paper and weighed at regular intervals. After 32 hours, the worms were gently massaged to void the remaining gut contents and weighed again, providing data to estimate the gut-clearance rate.
\[\frac{dP_{Weight}}{dt}=-\frac{\ln 2}{DT_{50,\text{gut}}}\ (P_{Weight}-P^{\infty}_{Weight}) \tag{3.5}\]
\[P_{Weight}=P^{\infty}_{Weight}+(100-P^{\infty}_{Weight})\ \exp\left(- \frac{\ln 2}{DT_{50,\text{gut}}}\times t\right)\]
With :

\(t\) : Time (h)
\(P_{Weight}\) : Percentage of initial weight (%, 0-100)
\(P^{\infty}_{Weight}\) : Percentage of initial weight when gut is fully emptied (%)
\(DT_{50,\text{gut}}\) : Half-life of the gut content (h)

For the model, we consider that weights measured after massage are weights measured after a significant amount of time has passed. Therefore, the corresponding percentages of initial weight inform \(P^{\infty}_{Weight}\).
The results are shown Figure 3.2.


Code
df_gut <- read_excel(here::here("data/Data_GUT.xlsx")) |> 
  mutate(
    ID = as.factor(ID)
  ) |> 
  group_by(ID) |> 
  mutate(
    Per_Weight = 100 * Weight / Weight[t == 0]
    ) |> 
  ungroup()

title: 3  Materiel & Methods
text:
3.6 Priors used
Large and weakly informative priors were assigned to the toxicokinetic rate constants in order to cover a wide range of plausible values. Specifically, the uptake (\(k_u\)), elimination (\(k_e\)), and inter-compartmental exchange rates (\(k_{periph}\)) were given log-uniform priors between 10\(^{−5}\) and 10\(^5\), ensuring that the posterior estimates would be primarily informed by the data rather than overly restrictive assumptions. For the growth process, truncated normal priors were specified for both the mean growth rate and its inter-individual variation, reflecting prior biological knowledge of adult earthworm growth while avoiding unrealistic values. Measurement processes were also integrated in the model: observed body weights were assigned truncated normal distributions to ensure positivity and consistency with biological constraints, while internal concentrations were assumed to follow truncated log-normal distributions, which are more appropriate for right-skewed data and guarantee non-negative values.

title: 3  Materiel & Methods
text:
3.7 Model implementation
Model calibration was performed with a bayesian inference software, MCSim, using 3 chains and 100 000. Posterior distributions were drawn using the last 10 000 iterations of each chain. Other statistical analyses were performed using the software R.






Pelosi, C., Thiel, P., Bart, S., Amossé, J., Jean-Jacques, J., Thoisy, J.-C., & Crouzet, O. (2021). The contributions of enchytraeids and earthworms to the soil mineralization process in soils with fungicide. Ecotoxicology, 30(9), 1910–1921. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10646-021-02452-z
